answer: The absorption rate in gas-film-controlled systems is proportional to the partial pressure driving force. In case (I), raising total pressure from 1 to 3 atm increases the partial pressure of methane from 0.15 to 0.45 atm, tripling the driving force and thus the absorption rate (a 200% increase). In case (II), the driving force is the partial pressure minus the vapor pressure (0.17 atm), which goes from (0.25 – 0.17) = 0.08 atm to (0.75 – 0.17) = 0.58 atm, a 625% increase, but none of the options match that; however, option J states no effect in the first case, which is incorrect, so the closest consistent choice is F, where the first case gains 50% (not correct) but the second has no effect—this is also wrong.
